channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: We need a “NO DOGS” sign please, 15th April
item.description:
Hi, A lot of people bring their dogs to the green square 1-17 Honiton Gardens, SE15 2AT and let them go to the toilet there, both during the day and evening not caring that there are children playing on the grass. We (us people who live on the square) constantly have to remove dog poop on both the grass and on the path. Please put up some “No dogs, £1000 fine” signs, thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Selden Road, New Cross Gate, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Some wood.dumped in street, 15th April
item.description:
Someone's left wood.perhapd a wardrobe in my road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Oaksford Avenue, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: One side of road is rarely cleaned, 15th April
item.description:
Once the small cleaning trucks used to clean the gutters on both sides of the top end of Buckingham Avenue (between Yorkland and Cumberland Avenues) regularly once every two weeks or so. However for a little while now (time unknown) it appears that they are only cleaning the side of the road with the odd house numbers and the even number side is being missed out. I heard them passing by the opposite side last Wednesday morning but they never returned to clean our side. Should an excuse be that there were too many parked vehicles I can confirm this part of Buckingham Avenue only had a couple of vehicles parked that morning leaving 95%+ of the road clear. This section of Buckingham Avenue rarely has many parked vehicles as most have off road parking. Most that are parked overnight are gone by 7.30am and I’ve never heard or seen a road cleaning vehicle at least until 9.00am.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cumberland Avenue, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Every time the lorry is coming to collect the rubish they leave mess behind i would say careless, 15th April
item.description:
Every time the lorry is coming to collect the rubish they leave a mess behind I would say careless about teaing sacks and lveing even big thing for no good reson

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Moresby Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mare Street, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Accumulated Litter in Shrub Area on Estate Needing Clearance, 15th April
item.description:
There is an bag of rubbish that has been dumped within the shrub area on the estate. The contents are spilling out and contributing to further litter in the surrounding area. This is unsightly mess and may attract additional dumping or pests if not addressed. Please arrange for removal of the waste and ensure the area is cleaned. Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Purbrook Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped shelving; dumped yellow barriers, 15th April
item.description:
The shelving has been there for a week. The barriers I believe belong to Clarion, who replaced some paving stones in Crouch Close in February. The barriers were left in Crouch Close by 20 Abbey Lane for 4wks before being moved outside of 21 Abbey Lane after I complained. That was 3wks ago and they've still not gone, so I don't think they are Clarion's and they've just moved them to no longer be "their problem" - Dumped.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20, Abbey Lane, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter Build-Up in Shrub Area Requiring Clearance and Maintenance, 15th April
item.description:
There is a build-up of litter and debris within the shrubbed area next to the walkways, including wine bottles, cans, and general waste in many of the areas of Purbrook Estate ( Purbrook Street/Tower Bridge Road )The areas appears not to have been cleaned for some time. This is creating an untidy environment and could attract further littering or pests. Please arrange for a litter pick and regular maintenance of this area. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Purbrook Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement., 15th April
item.description:
Washing machine dumped on pavement.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Exeter Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car stationary for 3 months, 15th April
item.description:
Car has several flat tyres; has not moved for months; filled with rubbish. Whether it is taxed or not or has MOT or not or is insured or not is not relevant. It’s meets guidance for an abandoned vehicle and needs dealing with by the council
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2A, Parkhurst Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damaged Brick Wall and Loose Slab Creating Safety Hazard, 15th April
item.description:
There is damage to the low brick wall next to the garage area, with part of the corner broken and crumbling. Loose masonry is visible, and a concrete slab has been displaced onto the ground nearby. This could pose a safety risk, particularly as the loose materials may become a trip hazard or deteriorate further. Please arrange for repair of the wall and removal or securing of the loose slab. Located near Tomson House ( St Saviours Estate, Bermondsey)

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Riley Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
